task_completion.description: Access the SEB Server and log in with the provided credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SEB Server dashboard or main interface is visible in the web browser, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the user management or accounts section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A list of users or the user management page is displayed in the SEB Server interface.

task_completion.description: Locate and open the account settings for 'Emily Chen' (emily.chen).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The edit profile or account settings page specifically for the user 'emily.chen' (Emily Chen) is open.
The timezone field or dropdown in the user's edit form is set to 'Asia/Tokyo'.

task_completion.description: Change the user's email address to 'emily.chen@university-tokyo.ac.jp'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The email input field in the user's edit form contains exactly 'emily.chen@university-tokyo.ac.jp'.
A success notification, confirmation message, or the user profile/list showing the updated email and timezone in a saved state (not actively being edited).

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a web browser displaying the SEB Server interface, rather than terminal commands like curl or mysql.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual SEB Server application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows the correct local URL (http://localhost:8080) and the genuine SEB Server UI is visible, not a text editor or mock file.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI to update the database directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing SQL queries or database client interfaces are used to alter the user's data.
